methodsAttempted.query: site:aau.edu.jo ("academic integrity" OR plagiarism OR "student discipline" OR "تعليمات الطلبة" OR "نظام تأديب" OR "الانتحال") "Amman Arab University"

targets.sourceTitle: تعليمات استخدام الذكاء الاصطناعي في العملية التعليمية في جامعة عمان العربية لسنة 2026

Do not crawl disallowed search/login/admin paths or non-official sources.

artifacts.sourceTitle: تعليمات استخدام الذكاء الاصطناعي في العملية التعليمية في جامعة عمان العربية لسنة 2026

artifacts.claimText: Amman Arab University lists an official 2026 document titled 'Instructions for using artificial intelligence in the Educational Process' on its Rules, Regulations & Instructions page.

AAU's 2026 AI educational-process instructions state that they regulate the use of AI tools and technologies in the university's educational and research process and apply to faculty members, students, and part-time lecturers at the university.

citation.citationTitle: تعليمات استخدام الذكاء الاصطناعي في العملية التعليمية في جامعة عمان العربية لسنة 2026

artifacts.claimText: AAU's AI instructions allow faculty members to use AI tools for educational content, learning activities, rubrics, and student-performance analysis, while requiring the faculty member to remain the course's primary scientific source and not rely fully on generated outputs without review.

citation.citationTitle: تعليمات استخدام الذكاء الاصطناعي في العملية التعليمية في جامعة عمان العربية لسنة 2026

artifacts.claimText: AAU's AI instructions allow students to use AI tools for self-learning, initial project and research ideas, and text or data analysis, but prohibit submitting AI outputs as-is, generating complete or nearly complete assignment/project answers without being able to explain them, or using tools that lead to cheating or breach academic integrity.

citation.citationTitle: تعليمات استخدام الذكاء الاصطناعي في العملية التعليمية في جامعة عمان العربية لسنة 2026

artifacts.claimText: AAU's AI instructions require students to document AI use by explicitly identifying the tool used and where and how it was used in assignments, research, and projects, while observing intellectual-property rights.

citation.citationTitle: تعليمات استخدام الذكاء الاصطناعي في العملية التعليمية في جامعة عمان العربية لسنة 2026

artifacts.claimText: AAU's AI instructions prohibit graduate students from using AI to generate ready-made chapters, results, or hypotheses, to generate questionnaires or fictitious data, or to rewrite paragraphs to hide plagiarism.

citation.citationTitle: تعليمات استخدام الذكاء الاصطناعي في العملية التعليمية في جامعة عمان العربية لسنة 2026

artifacts.claimText: AAU's AI instructions state that university legislation and decisions apply to anyone who violates the AI instructions or misuses AI tools in any form.

artifacts.notes: Narrow source-status claim supported by the official rules page listing the AI instructions document.
Claim limited to the PDF's stated purpose and affected groups.
Claim combines clauses 5 and 6 without extending beyond listed faculty use cases and conditions.
Claim reflects clauses 7 and 8 and avoids inventing approved tool names.
Documentation requirement is stated directly in clause 9.
Graduate-student prohibitions are stated directly in clause 11.
Consequence claim is limited to the PDF statement that existing university legislation and decisions apply.

artifacts.title: Amman Arab University 2026 AI Educational-Process Instructions
artifacts.summary: Official AAU source discovery found a 2026 Arabic PDF on AI use in the educational process, linked from the university's Rules, Regulations & Instructions page. The staged claims cover official source status, scope, faculty use conditions, student permitted and prohibited uses, student disclosure requirements, graduate-student prohibitions, and handling of misuse under existing university legislation and decisions.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The source PDF is Arabic; English snippets in the staged evidence are helper summaries only.
No approved/prohibited named AI tool list or AI-specific privacy/security rule was found in the official sources checked.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
